publication.title: Hazard Tree Identification and Mitigation
publication.publisher: USDA Forest Service, Pacific Southwest Region, Forest Health Protection
publication.geographic_scope: California and Region 5 National Forest System management context
publication.supersedes: All previous versions, including April 2012 Report RO-12-01

remote_object.retention_reason: The mixed-component 1.6 MB PDF is identified by exact official URL, headers, byte length, digest, and structural metadata. The repository retains the compact provenance and manually transformed corpus, not the binary.

embedded_metadata.title: Hazard Tree Identification and Mitigation, Region 5, USFS
embedded_metadata.author: fsdefaultUser
embedded_metadata.creator: Acrobat PDFMaker 22 for Word

pdf_structure.identity_rule: Named authors and publication identity come from the visible report cover. The generic embedded PDF author field is recorded but is not used as authorship metadata.

rights_review.basis: The report is issued by the USDA Forest Service Pacific Southwest Region as an official Forest Health Protection technical report. Eligible United States Government text is treated as presumptively public domain under 17 U.S.C. 105, while no blanket conclusion is made about embedded, derived, administrative, or cited third-party components.
rights_review.interpretation_of_absence: The absence of an extracted rights or credit statement is not evidence that every component is federally authored or independently reusable.
rights_review.included_treatment: Original manual paraphrase and synthesis of eligible federal explanatory text with printed-page citations and a change notice.
excluded_components: all numbered figures and figure artwork
expressive source table and scoring layout
maps, mapping examples, and location codes
cited standards, manuals, books, articles, and other third-party prose
species, disease, score, and action tables
any component whose authorship or rights were not independently established

components.description: wide cover graphic or agency-mark banner; one raster object occurrence
pre- and post-mitigation hazard-risk matrices; two raster panels
four potential-failure-zone diagrams for level ground, slope, tree parts, and lean; eight raster object occurrences including graphic fragments
included-bark formation illustration; one raster object occurrence
hazard score, potential category, and suggested-action table rendered as one raster image
hazard-tree evaluation form graphic; one raster object occurrence
evaluation-form or mapping graphic plus a raster rule fragment; two raster object occurrences
quick-list graphic fragment; one raster object occurrence within a two-page scoring layout
Survey123 application screenshot; one raster object occurrence

component_audit.audit_note: Raster object occurrence count is a PDF structural count, not a claim that each object is one distinct expressive work. Vector and text layouts were also reviewed by page and excluded with their parent component.

review_method.automation_boundary: pypdf text extraction was used only to navigate and verify all 40 pages, metadata, headings, captions, and candidate claims. PDF resource enumeration supported the component audit. The training Markdown was manually selected, paraphrased, reorganized, scoped, and checked; raw extraction was not emitted as training prose.
reviewed_regions: cover, authors, report number, revision, and table of contents
site prioritization, inspection frequency, and inspection context
all defect sections: cracks, unions, decay, fruiting bodies, cankers, dead parts, insects, roots, lean, and architecture
rating system and source Table 1 at exclusion-disposition level
all management and mitigation sections at exclusion-disposition level
monitoring, records, failure records, and specialist assistance
references and additional reading at exclusion-disposition level
Appendices 1 through 5 at page and disposition level
all six numbered figures, source Table 1, cover graphic, form and quick-list graphics, and application screenshot at component-disposition level

evidence_audit.central_uncertainty: It is impossible to predict all tree failures; a professional program can reduce but not eliminate risk.
evidence_audit.scope_gate: Region 5 and California Forest Service management guidance, not a universal species, disease, scoring, or arboriculture standard.
conceptual_distinctions: defect is a flaw that reduces structural strength
failure is mechanical breakage of a tree or tree part
target is a person or built asset that could be struck
loss is personal injury or monetary property damage
hazard combines tree-side failure concern with target-side exposure and consequence
rating system is an administrative prioritization tool rather than a physical capacity measurement
inspection_context: insect, disease, fire, and weather impacts
species, age, condition, and site history in local professional context
major storms, fire, reopening, construction, or other material change
retained_defect_families: decay, cavities, fruiting bodies, and cankers
biological attack as a contributor to death and decay
recent or changing lean versus long-compensated lean
retained_site_change_factors: heavy equipment, vehicle traffic, and concentrated foot traffic
root lifting, soil cracking, soil movement, and mounding
excluded_nonportable_claims: circumference, diameter, length, percentage, and distance cutoffs

retained_scope: failure, defect, hazard, target, and loss distinctions
defect families and interaction without scores
site, soil, disturbance, weather, and temporal-change context
Forest Health Protection, certified-arborist, and professional-arborist referral
excluded_material: mitigation, cutting, felling, removal, pruning, topping, cabling, bracing, and support instructions
universalized species, disease, rating, decay, size, or distance claims
figures, graphics, source tables, forms, maps, and screenshots

forbidden_derivations: working-load limit or safety factor from a hazard score
wrap, knot, direction, or component selection
anchor, backup, frame, or suspension-system design
safe location from a numerical failure zone
capacity from healthy foliage, corrected lean, or absence of visible defects
approval from one professional domain alone
required_professional_domains: qualified arboricultural assessment of the living tree, roots, site, defects, disease, and foreseeable tree failure modes
qualified rigging or structural assessment of the proposed load path, forces, dynamics, components, attachment effects, redundancies, and consequences
anchor_and_human_suspension_boundary.controlling_rule: The corpus provides no load-bearing approval. Tree-risk and load-path assessments are separate and both remain necessary for any contemplated use.

split_hygiene.document_disjoint_requirement: Assign FHP Report RO-22-01 and all derived Markdown or QA to one split before chunking or derivation.
